evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establish and enforce best practices in testing, documentation, and data quality - making these part of the standard development lifecycle
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing, documentation, data quality, and data contracts dominate the responsibilities with no velocity or shipping-speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Cybercrime is costing the world >$10 trillion annually
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: A cybersecurity SaaS company's internal analytics affect product and business decisions without explicit financial-reporting or regulatory-audit language in the responsibilities themselves.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Collaborate with Data Engineers on upstream data contracts and event schemas
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named, distinct Data Engineers team and existing semantic layer to evolve signal a growing, mid-maturity function rather than greenfield or fully mature.
evidence.jd_authorship: Model complex SaaS data by integrating product events, CRM (Salesforce), and support data into clean, well-defined fact and dimension models
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tools and data sources (Salesforce, dbt, MetricFlow, Cube) and concrete modeling scope show exactly what the role involves.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build and evolve our semantic layer - creating a reliable abstraction over our data that enables consistent KPI definitions and supports downstream consumers
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is internal data consumers across analytics, product, and AI use cases via the semantic layer.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the transformation layer in dbt; Establish and enforce best practices in testing, documentation, and data quality
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Owning the transformation layer and establishing/enforcing standards across the company are unambiguously strategic verbs.
evidence.ai_role: supports downstream consumers, including LLM-based analytics agents; interest in building data infrastructure that serves AI agents, not just BI tools
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds semantic-layer infrastructure explicitly consumed by LLM-based analytics agents, making this AI-enabler work.
evidence.testing_framing: Establish and enforce best practices in testing, documentation, and data quality - making these part of the standard development lifecycle
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is explicitly framed as something the candidate establishes and enforces, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Collaborate with Data Engineers on upstream data contracts and event schemas - ensuring raw data is structured in a way that supports scalable, reliable analytics
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data contracts and reliability are emphasized as engineering concerns without explicit compliance or stakeholder-trust framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and evolve our semantic layer
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of owning/building the transformation layer from scratch for new AI use cases and evolving an existing semantic layer.
